title: Psychotria camponutans
intro: Psychotria camponutans is a medicinal plant species native to tropical regions, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, malaria, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Psychotria camponutans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Psychotria camponutans is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and may also be found in the form of capsules or extracts, particularly in traditional medicine practices in Central and South America.
taxonomy: Psychotria camponutans is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which includes other species of plants commonly known as coffee, gardenias, and cinchonas. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse family of flowering plants, with over 13,000 known species. Psychotria camponutans is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The exact classification within the Rubiaceae family is not well-documented, but it is commonly placed in the subfamily Psychotrioideae.
